subject: Goal Chain lug type — distributed session coordination and claiming primitive
title: Goal Chain Lug Type + Claim Registry
what: Defines the chain lug type — a portable, resumable work-coordination unit that sits between an epic (strategic) and an implementation lug (atomic). A chain groups related goals into an execution sequence, allows an agent to claim it before planning (preventing races between concurrent sessions), and scopes the session plan to what the current model + token budget can complete. Uncompleted children are deferred cleanly to the next session. A claim registry (Supabase wai_claims table) enables multi-Ozi distributed claiming with TTL-based expiry; expired claims create investigation lugs as failure-learning signals rather than silent resets.
why: Without goal chains, two sessions can plan the same work independently (wasted tokens) or leave goals in an ambiguous partially-started state. The chain primitive makes multi-session and multi-agent coordination safe: claim before plan, plan within budget, execute, defer overflow, release cleanly. TTL expiry converts abandoned claims into learning signals. The Work Goals vs Spoke Goals distinction prevents strategic goals from being re-scoped implicitly inside a single session's context.
audience: AP (Autopilot) — claims chains during session startup
Navigator — queries available chains when building portfolio slice
Any agent that dispatches or resumes multi-session work
use_cases: Multi-session epic: AP claims spec-goal-chain-v1 for the morning wave, plans what fits in 100k tokens, defers overflow to next session cleanly
Multi-Ozi coordination: two Ozi instances see the same work queue; only the one that claims a chain proceeds — PK collision rejects the second
Resumable overnight run: Ozi claims a chain at 11pm, hits token limit, defers remaining children; next morning Ozi sees deferred_children and continues
Conflict prevention: file_scope in session_plan locks the files this session will touch — another session that would touch the same files sees the lock and waits
patterns: claim-before-plan: agent MUST claim chain before creating any implementation plan; unclaimed chains are available to any session
TTL expiry = learning signal: expired claims create an investigation lug with failure context, not a silent claim reset
session_plan scoping: planned_children = what fits in this session given model + budget; deferred_children = overflow for next session
Work Goals vs Spoke Goals: Work Goals are chain-level outcomes that persist across sessions; Spoke Goals are what this session will accomplish from the Work Goal — never collapse the two

chain_lug_fields.id: string — slug format matching chain name e.g. chain-herald-impl-wave1-v1
chain_lug_fields.chain_id: string — same as id, for query clarity in claim registry joins
chain_lug_fields.goal: string — the Work Goal this chain accomplishes (one sentence)
children: lug_id — ordered list of child lug IDs
completed_children: lug_id — children successfully completed in prior sessions
chain_lug_fields.ttl_hours: int — default 6; claim expires at claimed_at + ttl_hours

session_plan.model: string — model_id this session will use e.g. claude-haiku-4-5
session_plan.budget_tokens: int — token ceiling for this claim
planned_children: lug_id — children this session will attempt
deferred_children: lug_id — children deferred to next session
file_scope: path — files this session will touch; conflict lock

columns.expires_at: timestamptz GENERATED ALWAYS AS (claimed_at + ttl_hours * interval '1 hour') STORED

investigation_lug_on_ttl_expiry.title: Investigate abandoned claim: {chain_id}
investigation_lug_on_ttl_expiry.perceive: Read the expired claim record. Check session track for the claiming session. Identify failure mode.
investigation_lug_on_ttl_expiry.execute: Diagnose why the claim expired without completion. Update chain lug deferred_children to reflect actual state.
investigation_lug_on_ttl_expiry.verify: Chain lug status reset to open or deferred. Expired claim removed from claims table.

context.expired_claim: full claim record snapshot at expiry time

constraints: An agent MUST write its claim to the claims table BEFORE beginning implementation planning — planning without claiming is a protocol violation
Only one active claim per chain_id (PRIMARY KEY enforces this in Supabase; file lock enforces in local fallback)
A claim older than expires_at is abandoned — next agent may claim AFTER writing an investigation lug with the expired claim context
file_scope conflicts must be detected at claim time: if session_plan.file_scope overlaps with any active claim's file_scope, the session must wait or choose a different chain
completed_children[] is updated atomically at each child's closeout — never batch-update at end of session only
Work Goals live at the chain level and must not be re-scoped or narrowed inside a session's context without updating the chain lug
tests: Claim race: two Ozi instances simultaneously attempt to claim the same chain_id — only one succeeds; PK violation rejects the second
TTL expiry: create a claim with ttl_hours=0; run TTL sweep; verify investigation lug created with correct expired claim context; chain status reset to open
Deferred resume: chain has deferred_children=[X, Y]; next session claims it; X and Y appear in planned_children
file_scope conflict detection: chain A claims files [a.py, b.py]; chain B plans against a.py — conflict detected at claim time
Completed chain transition: move all children to completed_children[]; verify chain status transitions to completed
Work Goals vs Spoke Goals: session_plan.planned_children is a subset of children[] — verify parent goal not mutated when scope is narrowed

open_questions: wai_claims Supabase table not yet created
chain lug type not in validator

perceive: Read this spec fully. Check WAI-Spoke/WAI-State.json _work_queue for any existing chain items. Check if Supabase wai_claims table exists. Read spec-goal-queue-v1 if present for queue integration. Read spec-intra-agent-comms-v1 if present for coordination patterns.
execute: 1. Add 'chain' to valid lug types in lug schema validator. 2. Create Supabase wai_claims table per schema above. 3. Add claim() function to session startup tooling: write claim record before planning begins. 4. Add release() to session closeout: update completed_children[], write deferred_children, delete claim record. 5. Add TTL sweep to Gardener nightly: find expired claims, write investigation lugs, reset chain status. 6. Document Work Goals vs Spoke Goals in WAI-Guide.md with examples.
verify: 1. Create a test chain lug and claim it — verify row appears in wai_claims. 2. Second claim attempt — verify rejection. 3. Set ttl_hours=0, run TTL sweep — verify investigation lug created, chain reset to open. 4. Complete a child lug at closeout — verify it moves to completed_children[]. 5. Work Goals vs Spoke Goals section present in WAI-Guide.md with concrete example.
outcome: Spec audited. Supabase wai_claims table and chain lug type not yet implemented. Subagent noted 3 impl lugs needed but did not create them (files not found on disk — corrected by session-20260601-0005). Implementation requires: chain lug validator, claim() module, TTL sweep. Blocked on Supabase credentials + lug type registration.
